picks.rationale: The Seattle Mariners enter this game in excellent form, boasting an 8-2 straight-up record in their last 10 games and a 7-2 record when favored. Conversely, the Detroit Tigers have struggled as underdogs this season, with a 10-19 record. Seattle also holds a significant offensive advantage on the road, averaging 4.33 runs and 1.13 home runs per game compared to Detroit's 3.87 runs at home. While the moneyline has seen some movement towards Detroit, Seattle's overall superior form and offensive production make them a strong play.

The Milwaukee Brewers are currently in strong form, holding a 7-3 record over their last 10 games. They have already demonstrated their dominance in this series by defeating the Colorado Rockies 7-1 and 9-7 in extra innings. The Rockies, with a 24-41 record, are among the weakest teams in the league. Given Milwaukee's consistent performance and their previous decisive wins against Colorado, the Brewers are well-positioned to cover the -1.5 run line.

The St. Louis Cardinals are coming off a commanding 10-3 victory over the Cincinnati Reds in the previous game of this series. Key offensive player Lars Nootbaar also made a successful return to the Cardinals' lineup, contributing an RBI double in that win. The Cardinals have shown better recent form with a 5-5 record in their last 10 games. With Matthew Liberatore on the mound for St. Louis, they have a strong opportunity to secure another home victory against the Reds.

picks.game: Los Angeles Angels @ Los Angeles Dodgers
picks.rationale: The Los Angeles Dodgers have been dominant in this series, securing wins of 1-0 and 9-2 in the first two games against the Angels. The Angels are struggling, holding a 4-6 record in their last 10 games, and are missing key offensive talent like Jorge Soler due to injury. Additionally, Dodgers catcher Will Smith, who was scratched on June 6, is expected to return to the lineup, further strengthening their offense. Given the Dodgers' superior form and the Angels' current struggles, covering the -1.5 spread at even odds presents excellent value.

The Philadelphia Phillies are in strong recent form, having won 7 of their last 10 games. Their offense is potent, led by Kyle Schwarber, who boasts 23 home runs and 40 RBIs, and Brandon Marsh with an impressive .335 batting average. On the pitching side, Cristopher Sánchez has been excellent with a 1.46 ERA and 103 strikeouts. While the moneyline is heavily juiced, the Phillies' strong performance, offensive firepower, and home-field advantage against the Chicago White Sox make this a high-confidence moneyline pick.
